title: cleanupOrphans under-records edge tombstones orphaned by its own node deletion

findingDetails.evidence: recordDanglingEdgeTombstones() at 1508 records only edges already dangling (source/target NOT IN code_nodes). Orphan nodes are still present then; they are deleted at 1520-1522, and edges attached to them become dangling and are removed by the sweep at 1525-1529 with no tombstone. The explicit node-tombstone INSERT (1512-1517) covers nodes but not these side-effect edges.
findingDetails.why: The feature-catalog doc (code-graph-status.md:24) claims tombstones capture 'deletion lineage'; under SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_TOMBSTONES=true, byReason['cleanup_orphans'] silently omits edges deleted as a consequence of orphan-node removal, so the audit trail is incomplete. Other delete paths (replaceNodes/removeFile) record edge tombstones for affected symbols BEFORE deleting; cleanupOrphans records before the nodes vanish, so the predicate misses them.
findingDetails.recommendation: Record edge tombstones for edges referencing the soon-to-be-deleted orphan nodes BEFORE the node DELETE (e.g. recordEdgeTombstonesForSymbols over symbol_ids whose file_id NOT IN code_files), mirroring removeFile's ordering, OR re-run recordDanglingEdgeTombstones after the node delete and before the edge sweep.

title: cleanupOrphans is not wrapped in a transaction unlike removeFile/replaceNodes/replaceEdges

findingDetails.evidence: cleanupOrphans (1505-1532) issues tombstone INSERTs, DELETE code_nodes (1520-1522) and DELETE code_edges (1525-1529) as separate auto-committed statements with no d.transaction() wrapper. removeFile (1140), replaceNodes (929) and replaceEdges (980) all wrap their record+delete in d.transaction(); removeFile's wrapper is the explicit CG-003 fix.
findingDetails.why: A crash between statements leaves an inconsistent intermediate (orphan nodes deleted, dangling edges not yet swept) and, on retry, re-records the same dangling edges producing duplicate tombstone rows. The CG-003 atomicity guarantee applied to removeFile was not extended to this sibling cleanup path.
findingDetails.recommendation: Wrap the cleanupOrphans body in d.transaction(() => { ... })() so tombstone recording and the node/edge deletes commit atomically, matching removeFile/replaceNodes/replaceEdges.
